_meta.purpose: Validation whitelist for the planner's free-elective format check (3-letter prefix + 4-digit number, optionally followed by C/L). Presence of a prefix here means it is a real UF/SCNS course prefix as of the source catalogs used -- it does NOT mean every number under that prefix exists, and it does NOT mean the specific course is BME-approved. It is the first filter in a three-step check: format -> known prefix + number>=2000 -> (separately) approved-elective list.
_meta.source: Primary source: University of Florida 2011-12 Undergraduate Catalog 'Index to Course Prefixes' (https://archive.catalog.ufl.edu/ugrad/1112/courses/Pages/prefix.html) -- found by Chris, more current and far more complete than the 2003-04 archive originally used. Short subject titles (where available) carried over from the 2003-04 archive for readability. Two additions confirmed newer than the 2011-12 catalog and already in active use in the planner's own course data: EGS (Engineering Studies/Leadership) and MDU (Medicine, intro-to-professions courses). Cross-checked: every prefix already used in core-courses.json/approved-electives.json is present.
_meta.known_limitations: Department names reflect the 2011-12 catalog and may have since been renamed/reorganized (e.g., department mergers) even where the prefix itself is unchanged -- SCNS prefixes themselves rarely retire. This is not a live scrape of the current (2025-26) catalog department-by-department; a small number of prefixes created since 2011-12 may still be missing beyond the two already added. Recommend periodic spot-check the same way core-courses.json/approved-electives.json already get reviewed.
